PLAINTIFF COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN WORD FORMAT TO OPPOSING COUNSEL AND TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT KLGALLO@DISCOVERYREFEREE.COM. MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Nov-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOSHUA A. RIDLESS, Esq. of Ridless Law Office (Tel: 415-614-2600) for plaintiff;
Atty. TREVIS J. NIEMEYER, Esq. of Berding & Weil LLP (Tel: 925-838-2090) for defendant Beacon Commercial Owners Association;
Atty. JACKSON L. STOGNER, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney for defendant Beacon Residential Community Association.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TEL-ONE NETWORK SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES.

All parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and rules as follows:

Plaintiff's instructions listed in paragraphs 14 - 17 are stricken. All requirements in responding to a document demand and producing documents are in CCP 2031.210-240 and CCP 2031.280.

Defendant's General Comments are stricken. The Discovery Act does not authorize a preamble, preliminary statement or general objections. The Discovery Act requires the party to respond in writing to each request for production of document whether it is a response or an objection. See CCP 2031.210-240.

Parties have now stipulated to the definitions of "You" and "Your."

Request #1: Objections on the grounds of compound and equal access are overruled. Objections on the grounds of privilege must be listed in a privilege log. Defendant is obligated to perform a diligent search and reasonable inquiry including its business affiliate Beacon Residential in responding to the document request. Defendant is obligated to diligent search and reasonable inquiry including any business affiliates it has in its care, custody, and control in responding to the document request. Defendant to provide a further response pursuant to CCP 2031.210-240 and produce documents pursuant to CCP 2031.280(a) by December 10, 2021.

Request #2, 3 and 4: Objections on compound and equal access are overruled. Objection regarding the scope of time is sustained. Objections on the grounds of privilege must be listed in a privilege log. Defendant is obligated to perform a diligent search and reasonable inquiry including its business affiliate Beacon Residential in responding to the document request. Defendant to provide a further response pursuant to CCP 2031.210-240 and produce documents pursuant to CCP 2031.280(a) by December 10, 2021, limiting the time to 2014 to the present.

Request #5: Objections on the grounds of vague and ambiguous are overruled. Defendant is obligated to perform a diligent search and reasonable inquiry including its business affiliate Beacon Residential in responding to the document request. Defendant to provide a further response pursuant to CCP 2031.210-240 and produce documents pursuant to CCP 2031.280(a) by December 10, 2021.

The Court makes no adjudication as to whether MNetworks is under defendant's care, custody, and control.

The Court makes no ruling on sanctions request at this time. The matter is continued to December 17, 2021 for a compliance hearing and the issue of plaintiff's request for sanctions.

The Court invites the parties to request an informal discovery conference with the Court to occur 12/6/21, 12/7/21, or 12/8/21.

Plaintiff counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order in Word format to opposing counsel and to the Judge Pro Tem at Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
